design_philosophy.core_loop: Arcade racing with combat elements; player races opponent vehicles while navigating track, with ability to engage in vehicle-to-vehicle combat similar to Road Rash
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing speed maintenance with tactical combat against rival racers on the track
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: High-speed street racing with the ability to physically interfere with competitors
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Deliberate cartoon aesthetic and Road Rash-inspired combat integration into racing, emphasizing chaotic multiplayer racing over simulation or pure speed optimization

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multitap support indicates 4-player simultaneous racing capability, suggesting difficulty scaling through player count and competitive interaction

replayability_hooks: Multitap support for up to 4 simultaneous players
Cartoon aesthetic encouraging casual repeated play

world_building_techniques: Cartoon art style establishes lighthearted tone distinct from realistic racing

environmental_storytelling: Cartoon visual presentation signals arcade/party game positioning rather than simulation

sources_used: tcrf - Street Racer (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
